title: Ensure that the proxy file ownership is set to root:root

input_row.title: Ensure that the proxy file ownership is set to root:root
input_row.description: Ensure that the proxy file ownership is set to root:root.
input_row.rationale: The proxy file controls various parameters that set the behavior of the kube-proxy service in the worker node. You should set its file ownership to maintain the integrity of the file. The file should be owned by root:root.
input_row.audit: Run the below command (based on the file location on your system) on the each worker node. For example, stat -c %U:%G /etc/kubernetes/proxy Verify that the ownership is set to root:root.
input_row.remediation: Run the below command (based on the file location on your system) on the each worker node. For example, chown root:root /etc/kubernetes/proxy Impact: None Default Value: By default, proxy file ownership is set to root:root. References: 1. https://kubernetes.io/docs/admin/kube-proxy/   183 | P a g e
input_row.impact: None Default Value: By default, proxy file ownership is set to root:root.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Manual

**APPROACH DECISION:**
This control requires verification of the file ownership of the kube-proxy configuration file (e.g., /etc/kubernetes/proxy) on each worker node. Kubernetes APIs and kubectl do not expose host-level file ownership or permissions for files outside of containerized workloads. The audit instructions specify using the stat command on the host OS, which requires direct access to the node filesystem. Therefore, this check cannot be automated via Kubernetes APIs or kubectl and must be performed manually.

**MANUAL STEPS:**
1. SSH into each Kubernetes worker node.
2. Run the following command to check the ownership of the kube-proxy configuration file (replace the path if your proxy file is located elsewhere):
   ```
   stat -c %U:%G /etc/kubernetes/proxy
   ```
3. Verify that the output is:
   ```
   root:root
   ```
4. If the ownership is not root:root, remediate by running:
   ```
   chown root:root /etc/kubernetes/proxy
   ```
5. Repeat for all worker nodes in the cluster to ensure compliance.

**SOURCE:** CIS KUBERNETES 1.6 BENCHMARK V1.0.0 ARCHIVE
